title: narcissus calcicola subsp. transtaganus
intro: Narcissus calcicola subsp. transtaganus, also known as the Transtaganus Narcissus or Tenerife narcissus, is a subspecies of the Narcissus calcicola plant, which is a type of daffodil native to the Canary Islands. The plant's bulbs have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which are believed to aid in the treatment of various health conditions, including skin infections and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Narcissus calcicola subsp. transtaganus include its ability to reduce inflammation, alleviate symptoms of colds and flu, and even exhibit antiviral properties. The plant is typically prepared for medicinal use by drying or infusing its bulbs in herbal teas, tinctures, or as a topical application, although its use should be strictly under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
